name: Eddie Vinson
description: American jump blues, jazz, bebop and R&B alto saxophonist and blues shouter (1917–1988)
abstract: Eddie "Cleanhead" Vinson (born Edward L. Vinson Jr.; December 18, 1917 – July 2, 1988) was an American jump blues, jazz, bebop and R&B alto saxophonist and blues shouter.

works.t: Call It Stormy Monday (But Tuesday Is Just as Bad)

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Love
about_quotes.evidence: And I used to go down there and join them every day... And Eddie Vinson played a vowel trombone you know. And he drilled me through what we were going to play... So they used to hear us practicing over there so they hired us one Saturday night to give a dance out there.

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: The record we made that day is called Railroad Porter Blues... On the first date, Eddie sang it and I accompanied him. On my date, I sang it and Eddie accompanied me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: The record we made that day is called Railroad Porter Blues... On the first date, Eddie sang it and I accompanied him. On my date, I sang it and Eddie accompanied me.

about_quotes.speaker: Arnett Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: In Milt Larkin's band, there was Eddie... I did recordings with Eddy

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions 'Cleanhead' Eddie Vinson was playing alto, but the interviewee (Sam Jones) does not respond with any personal anecdote, memory, or statement indicating a direct interaction or relationship with Eddie Vinson.

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny St. Cyr
about_quotes.evidence: Well, Jimmy Brown was with the Golden Rule until then when they organized the Olympia band, he played with the band, he played with the band, and there was Tiet Rochon, Freddie Capard, Big Eye Louie, Eddie Vinson, trombone
